# Specification — DEL-019-04: Construction Quality Control

**Document type:** Specification (normative)
**Deliverable ID:** DEL-019-04_QC-Management
**Generated by:** 4_DOCUMENTS Pass 1+2; enriched Pass 3
**Date:** 2026-02-26

---

## Scope

### Included

This deliverable covers the implementation and ongoing oversight of the construction quality control (QC) program for the WDMLRL Maintenance Shop Addition project (AB-2026-01424). It encompasses:

- Development and approval of a written QC Plan.
- Establishment and execution of an inspection schedule aligned with construction phases.
- Documentation and execution of material and systems testing protocols.
- Operation of a deficiency identification, tracking, and remediation-verification system.
- QC status reporting through weekly coordination meetings (DEL-019-02).
- Quality assurance oversight over all subcontractors engaged on site.
- Coordination of all inspection requests with the County project representative.
- Provision of inspection reports to the County.

(Source: _CONTEXT.md — Scope Definition; RFP §1.0, §3.3.2, §4.7.5; SOW-0089)

### Excluded

- Permitting and regulatory inspection coordination (PKG-009 scope).
- Commissioning and closeout quality verification (PKG-020 scope).
- Bonding and warranty obligations (PKG-021 scope).
- OH&S Program establishment — that is the subject of DEL-019-01; this deliverable draws authority from that program but does not duplicate it.

---

## Requirements

### REQ-QC-01: QC Plan — Existence, Content, and Approval

The Proponent shall develop a written QC Plan. The plan shall be documented and approved prior to commencement of construction activities. The QC Plan shall, at minimum, address:

1. Project QC objectives and scope.
2. Roles and responsibilities (Project Manager, QC Manager, subcontractor QC responsibilities).
3. Inspection hold-point structure aligned with construction phases.
4. Material quality control procedures (receipt verification, testing requirements by material type).
5. Deficiency identification, documentation, assignment, and close-out process.
6. Communication processes for deficiency correction (how deficiencies are reported, to whom, within what timeframe).
7. Reporting cadence and format for weekly coordination meetings (DEL-019-02).
8. How the QC program extends to subcontractors.

**Basis:** RFP §4.7.5; _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria; Procedure Phase A, Step A2 (minimum content list).
**Verification:** Approved QC Plan document on file; content reviewed against minimum content list above.
**Note (Pass 3 — F-001):** The minimum content list above aligns REQ-QC-01 with the detailed content specified in Procedure A2, resolving the prior weak statement where the requirement text was broad enough to permit varying interpretations.

---

### REQ-QC-02: Inspection Schedule

The Proponent shall establish an inspection schedule aligned with construction phases. The schedule shall identify inspection points for all significant construction activities.

**Basis:** _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria ("All inspections completed per schedule"); RFP §3.3.2 (County representative to be present for all inspections).
**Verification:** Inspection schedule document; inspection completion records.

---

### REQ-QC-03: County Representative Attendance at Inspections

The County project representative must be present for all inspections and shall be provided with a copy of all complete inspection reports.

**Basis:** RFP §3.3.2 (verbatim requirement).
**Verification:** Inspection reports issued to County representative; attendance records.

---

### REQ-QC-04: Inspection Request Submission

All inspection requests shall be submitted by the Proponent.

**Basis:** RFP §3.3.2.
**Verification:** Inspection request log.

---

### REQ-QC-05: Testing Protocols and Acceptance Criteria

The Proponent shall document testing protocols for materials and systems. Protocols shall be executed and results shall be documented. Each testing protocol shall define acceptance criteria (pass/fail thresholds) specific to the material or system type being tested.

**Basis:** _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria ("Testing protocols executed and results documented"); RFP §4.7.5.
**Verification:** Testing protocol documents include defined acceptance criteria; test result records demonstrate evaluation against those criteria.
**Note (Pass 3 — B-001):** Acceptance criteria are TBD pending IFC specification completion. The QC Plan shall either include acceptance criteria directly or reference the applicable IFC specification sections that define them for each material/system type.

---

### REQ-QC-06: Deficiency Tracking, Severity Classification, and Remediation

The Proponent shall operate a deficiency tracking system. All deficiencies identified during inspections or testing shall be:
(a) classified by severity (critical / major / minor) with differentiated response timelines and escalation triggers per severity category;
(b) documented upon identification;
(c) assigned for remediation;
(d) verified as corrected before closure.

Final work must meet specification requirements before the Proponent requests a final inspection or Construction Completion Certificate.

**Basis:** _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria; RFP §2.10.4 (Owner notification of deficiencies, Proponent remediation obligation); RFP §2.14.1 (Proponent self-review before requesting CCC).
**Verification:** Deficiency log with severity classification and status tracking; closed deficiency sign-off records; differentiated response timelines documented in QC Plan.
**Note (Pass 3 — E-003):** Severity classification scheme (definitions of critical/major/minor, response timelines, escalation triggers) is TBD — to be defined in the QC Plan. The classification should enable prioritized response and category-based reporting.

---

### REQ-QC-07: Subcontractor QC Oversight

The Proponent, as Prime Contractor, shall extend QC oversight to all subcontractors performing work on site. Subcontractor work shall be subject to the same inspection and sign-off rigor as direct Proponent work.

**Basis:** RFP §2.15 (Prime Contractor designation applies whenever Proponent's personnel, equipment, or subcontractors are on site); decomposition PKG-019 scope description.
**Verification:** Subcontractor inspection records demonstrating equivalent inspection rigor; reference to subcontractor performance evaluations (DEL-019-03).
**Note (Pass 3 — C-001):** The verification approach now explicitly requires demonstrated conformance at the same standard as direct Proponent work, resolving the prior gap where "subcontractor inspection records" alone did not specify the conformance demonstration threshold.

---

### REQ-QC-08: QC Reporting

QC status and deficiency reports shall be presented at weekly coordination meetings (DEL-019-02).

**Basis:** _DEPENDENCIES.md (declared dependency: DEL-019-02 is the QC reporting forum).
**Verification:** Weekly meeting minutes referencing QC status items.

---

### REQ-QC-09: Conformance to IFC Drawings

All construction work subject to QC oversight shall conform to Issued for Construction (IFC) drawings signed and stamped by a Professional Engineer licensed in Alberta.

**Basis:** RFP §3.3.2.
**Verification:** QC inspection records reference IFC drawing revision; non-conformances documented when deviations identified.

---

### REQ-QC-10: Compliance with Alberta Safety Codes

The building and all construction activities shall comply with all applicable Alberta Safety Codes.

**Basis:** RFP §3.3.2 ("The proposed building must adhere to all Alberta Safety Codes").
**Verification:** Safety Code inspection reports and approvals.
**Note:** Specific applicable code editions are TBD (not enumerated in available sources). The QC Plan author or design team shall enumerate applicable code editions before construction commencement (see warranted item A-003).

---

### REQ-QC-11: QC Manager Assignment and Qualifications

A QC Manager shall be assigned to the project. The QC Manager is responsible for daily inspections, testing coordination, and deficiency documentation. The QC Manager shall meet minimum qualification requirements as defined in the QC Plan.

**Basis:** _CONTEXT.md — Key Roles; _STATUS.md — Outstanding Items.
**Verification:** QC Plan includes named QC Manager; QC Plan defines and QC Manager meets minimum qualification requirements (e.g., relevant experience, certifications, or training).
**Note (Pass 3 — B-003):** Minimum qualification requirements for the QC Manager are TBD — to be defined in the QC Plan or in the Proponent's proposal. No specific qualification standard is stated in accessible sources.

---

### REQ-QC-12: Guarantee Period Obligations

The Proponent guarantees all materials furnished and work performed for the construction period plus two (2) years post-CCC. Defects identified by the Owner during the guarantee period shall be rectified within ten (10) days of written instruction.

**Basis:** RFP §2.10.2, §2.10.6.
**Verification:** Written remediation confirmation within 10-day window; Owner sign-off on corrected work.

---

### REQ-QC-13: QC Plan Approval Authority

The QC Plan approval chain shall be defined. The QC Plan shall specify whether approval is internal (Project Manager), external (County), or both, and shall identify the approval authority by role.

**Basis:** _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria ("Quality control plan documented and approved"); RFP §4.7.5.
**Verification:** QC Plan identifies approval authority; approval signatures on file.
**Note (Pass 3 — A-001):** Approval authority is TBD — not explicitly stated in accessible sources. Procedure Step A3 currently defers this determination. This requirement ensures the ambiguity is resolved during QC Plan development.

---

### REQ-QC-14: Measurement and Test Method Standards

Testing protocols shall reference applicable measurement and test method standards (e.g., ASTM, CSA test methods) to ensure reliable and repeatable measurements across inspectors and test events.

**Basis:** RFP §4.7.5 (quality control management); ASSUMPTION: standard construction QC practice requires referenced test methods for measurement consistency.
**Verification:** Testing protocol documents reference applicable test method standards for each test type.
**Note (Pass 3 — B-002):** Specific applicable test method standards are TBD pending IFC specification completion. The QC Plan shall reference the applicable standards once IFC specifications identify materials and systems requiring testing.

---

### REQ-QC-15: QC Performance Metrics

The QC program shall define quantitative performance metrics or KPIs to enable assessment of QC program effectiveness. Metrics should include, at minimum, deficiency closure rate targets and inspection completion percentage targets.

**Basis:** _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria (multiple performance-oriented criteria); ASSUMPTION: quantitative metrics are standard practice for QC program assessment.
**Verification:** QC Plan includes defined KPIs with target values; periodic reporting demonstrates measurement against targets.
**Note (Pass 3 — A-004):** Specific KPI targets are TBD — to be defined in the QC Plan. The requirement ensures that QC program performance is assessed on a quantitative basis rather than solely through binary completion checks.

---

### REQ-QC-16: Construction Completion Conformance Determination

A formal conformance determination process shall be defined for rendering the final pass/fail verdict on overall QC program completion at construction completion. The process shall identify who has authority to render the final conformance verdict and how disputed conformance findings are resolved.

**Basis:** RFP §2.14.1 (Proponent self-review before CCC request); RFP §2.14.2 (Owner final inspection); _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria.
**Verification:** QC Plan defines the conformance determination process; conformance verdict documented at construction completion.
**Note (Pass 3 — D-001):** The prior framework relied on zero open deficiencies as the sole indicator without addressing disputed conformance findings or defining who renders the conclusive verdict.

---

### REQ-QC-17: Third-Party Testing Laboratory Qualifications

Where testing is performed by a third-party laboratory, the laboratory shall hold applicable accreditation (e.g., ISO 17025 or equivalent) for the test methods being performed.

**Basis:** ASSUMPTION: standard construction QC practice requires accredited testing laboratories for reliable results; RFP §4.7.5 (quality control management implies qualified testing).
**Verification:** Third-party laboratory accreditation certificates on file; accreditation scope covers test methods performed.
**Note (Pass 3 — X-005):** This requirement applies only when third-party testing is used. Whether third-party testing will be required is TBD pending IFC specification completion.

---

### REQ-QC-18: Inspection Personnel Competency

Personnel conducting inspections (QC Manager or delegates) shall be verified as competent to perform the specific inspections required. Competency verification shall be completed before the individual conducts inspections independently.

**Basis:** ASSUMPTION: standard construction QC practice requires competent inspection personnel; RFP §2.15 (Prime Contractor oversight obligations imply competent personnel).
**Verification:** Competency verification records on file for each inspection personnel; verification completed before first independent inspection.
**Note (Pass 3 — F-002):** No specific competency standard is stated in accessible sources. The QC Plan shall define minimum competency criteria for inspection personnel.

---

## Standards

| Standard / Code | Applicability | Availability |
|---|---|---|
| CCDC 14-2013 Design-Build Stipulated Price Contract | Governing contract form for the project; QC-related provisions (inspection rights, deficiency obligations, guarantee provisions) to be verified against contract text | Referenced in RFP §2.7; full text location TBD (see warranted item E-001) |
| Alberta Occupational Health and Safety Act (OHS Act) | Prime Contractor obligations and site safety — directly linked to QC/oversight program; specific sections (e.g., Part 37 re Prime Contractors) to be cited | Referenced in RFP §2.15 via Health and Safety agency designation; specific clause location TBD (see warranted item A-002) |
| Alberta Safety Codes Act and applicable Safety Codes | Building and construction compliance; specific code editions to be enumerated by QC Plan author or design team | RFP §3.3.2; specific editions TBD (see warranted item A-003) |
| Prompt Payment and Construction Lien Act (Alberta) | Governs lien holdback (10%) and payment terms | RFP §2.13.2; specific clause location TBD |
| ASSUMPTION: CSA and NBC standards | ASSUMPTION: likely applicable for materials and construction quality based on project type and Alberta jurisdiction; specific standards not enumerated in accessible sources | location TBD |
| ASSUMPTION: ASTM, CSA test method standards | ASSUMPTION: likely applicable for material testing (e.g., concrete, soils, steel); specific test method standards TBD pending IFC specifications (see warranted item B-002) | location TBD |

## Verification

| Requirement | Verification Approach |
|---|---|
| REQ-QC-01 (QC Plan — content and approval) | Review approved QC Plan document; verify minimum content list coverage |
| REQ-QC-02 (Inspection schedule) | Review schedule; cross-check inspection completion records |
| REQ-QC-03 (County attendance) | Review inspection reports for County representative acknowledgement |
| REQ-QC-04 (Inspection requests) | Review inspection request log |
| REQ-QC-05 (Testing protocols and acceptance criteria) | Review protocol documents for defined acceptance criteria; review test result records |
| REQ-QC-06 (Deficiency tracking and severity classification) | Audit deficiency log for severity classification; verify differentiated response timelines; verify all items closed before CCC request |
| REQ-QC-07 (Subcontractor QC — equivalent rigor) | Review subcontractor inspection records for equivalent inspection rigor; cross-reference DEL-019-03 performance evaluations |
| REQ-QC-08 (QC reporting) | Review weekly meeting minutes for QC agenda items |
| REQ-QC-09 (IFC conformance) | Cross-check inspection records against current IFC drawing revision |
| REQ-QC-10 (Alberta Safety Codes) | Safety Code inspection reports and permit sign-offs |
| REQ-QC-11 (QC Manager assignment and qualifications) | QC Plan includes named QC Manager; qualification requirements defined and met |
| REQ-QC-12 (Guarantee period) | Documented remediation records within 10-day window; Owner written sign-off |
| REQ-QC-13 (QC Plan approval authority) | Approval authority identified in QC Plan; approval signatures on file |
| REQ-QC-14 (Measurement and test method standards) | Testing protocols reference applicable test method standards |
| REQ-QC-15 (QC performance metrics) | QC Plan includes KPIs with targets; periodic reports demonstrate measurement |
| REQ-QC-16 (Conformance determination) | QC Plan defines conformance determination process; verdict documented |
| REQ-QC-17 (Third-party lab qualifications) | Accreditation certificates on file (when third-party testing used) |
| REQ-QC-18 (Inspection personnel competency) | Competency verification records on file before independent inspections |

---

## Documentation

The following artifacts are anticipated as outputs of this deliverable:

1. **QC Plan** — written, approved, filed prior to construction commencement; includes minimum content per REQ-QC-01, approval authority per REQ-QC-13, severity classification scheme per REQ-QC-06, KPIs per REQ-QC-15, and conformance determination process per REQ-QC-16.
2. **Inspection Schedule** — phased schedule of inspection points.
3. **Inspection Reports** — completed per inspection event using a standardized inspection report template (template TBD — see Guidance warranted item C-002); copies provided to County representative.
4. **Inspection Request Log** — record of all submitted inspection requests.
5. **Testing Protocols** — documented protocols per material/system type, including acceptance criteria (REQ-QC-05) and referenced test method standards (REQ-QC-14).
6. **Test Result Records** — completed test results for all protocols executed; evaluated against defined acceptance criteria.
7. **Deficiency Log** — running log of identified deficiencies with severity classification (critical/major/minor), status (open/closed), and differentiated response timelines.
8. **Deficiency Close-Out Sign-Off Records** — evidence of verified remediation.
9. **QC Status Reports** — presented at weekly coordination meetings (DEL-019-02); includes KPI reporting per REQ-QC-15.
10. **QC Manager Assignment Record** — named QC Manager confirmed in writing; qualification verification on file (REQ-QC-11).
11. **Inspection Personnel Competency Records** — competency verification for all inspection personnel (REQ-QC-18).
12. **Third-Party Lab Accreditation Records** — accreditation certificates when third-party testing is used (REQ-QC-17).

(Source: _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria; _STATUS.md — Outstanding Items; RFP §4.7.5, §3.3.2; Pass 3 enrichments)
